scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: Meets all pass criteria: 7 rules extracted with individual confidence/invariant ratings, 5+ clarifying questions surfaced as escalations, 'demand' explicitly called out as undefined (first escalation), acceptance_criteria array is empty (no Given/When/Then written), overall confidence.level is 30 (<=40), and no specific values were invented — all numbers are flagged as UNDEFINED.

observations.text: Extracted 7 business rules, each with invariant classification and justification — exceeds the 5-rule minimum
5 formal escalations covering demand definition, guardrail values, fallback strategy, regulatory compliance, and price-lock semantics
Demand ambiguity is the #1 escalation with concrete options and a clear recommendation
Acceptance criteria array is intentionally empty with an explicit rejection reason explaining why criteria would be fabrication
Confidence level of 30 with detailed reasoning about why the input is insufficient
8 edge cases identified, each marked UNDEFINED where the spec provides no answer
No values invented — guardrail bounds, thresholds, and percentages are consistently flagged as undefined
BR-6 (price must be positive) is a strong invariant extraction that demonstrates domain reasoning beyond the literal input

The testable_assertion fields on rules reference undefined thresholds (e.g., 'high-demand threshold') which could be misread as implying those thresholds exist — though each rule's invariant_justification clarifies they are policy choices
